name: Menudo by Patricia Hill
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the tripe into bite-sized pieces.
2. Soften the dried mild red chile in hot water.
3. Remove the stem and seeds (if desired) from the chile, then mince.
4. Add everything (onion, garlic, softened chile, salt, and pepper) to a crock pot containing enough water to cover the bone/feet.
5. Cook on low for 12-14 hours until tender.
6. Strain the broth through a fine-mesh strainer into a bowl or pan.
7. On day two, add the tripe (preferably in tiny pieces) and any remaining bones/feet to the strained broth.
8. Return any leftover broth from day one if desired.
9. Cook on low for another 12-14 hours until the tripe is tender.
10. Serve hot with a side of hominy, if using.
Note: The calf's foot adds richness to the broth. You can use pig's feet or marrow bones instead. Adding carrots and celery stalks enhances flavor but is optional. Some cooks prefer larger chunks for tripe, while others like it finely chopped. Many add coriander or additional green chile for extra spice.
